protocol.name: ext_input_observation_v1

interface.name: ext_input_observation_manager_v1
interface.description: This interface permits a surface to observe user input, even when the surface does not have the input device's focus.

request.name: create_observer

description.summary: Creates an observer for the surface.

request.description:
Creates a position observation instance for pointer position relative to `surface`.
The seat associated with the surface must have (or had) WL_SEAT_CAPABILITY_POINTER capabilitiey. Otherwise, WL_DISPLAY_ERROR_INVALID_METHOD is raised.

interface.name: ext_input_observer_v1
interface.description:
Permits observing input devices with respect to the associated surface.
Status of input devices will be shared only when the surface is mapped. If the surface is destroyed, the server will destroy any associated observer, too.

event.name: pointer_position
event.description:
Reports the pointer position relative to the surface.
If the compositor displays the surface more than once, it may share the pointer position relative to each instance of the surface, identified by the `instance` argument.
The position is reported relative to the surface's edge vectors, and irrespective of whether the surface has input focus or whether the device's position is in- or outside of the surface area.
The relative positions will be reported as signed 16:16 fixpoint values. Positions within the surface will also be reported, with values in the interval [0, 1) for both relative_x and relative_y.
